capabilities: Ensure raw data ingestion pipelines are robust, scalable, and maintain data integrity for machine learning applications.

procedure.action: Assert incoming data against a predefined schema using JSON Schema draft-07; log validation errors with field paths to a centralized error tracking system.

Deduplicate incoming data by applying a hash-based comparison on key fields; store unique records in a temporary staging area before final processing using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Partition data by date and key attributes using a consistent hashing algorithm; ensure partitions are balanced by monitoring partition size distribution using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Write validated and deduplicated data to a data lakehouse using Apache Parquet format; ensure atomic writes by leveraging transactional capabilities of the storage system.
Invoke a monitoring system to track data ingestion metrics such as throughput and error rates; alert when error rate exceeds 0.5% over a 10-minute window.

summary: Procedure for designing raw data ingestion pipelines in ML, focusing on schema validation, deduplication, and partitioning.
title: Raw Data Ingestion Pipeline Design for ML

failureConditions: Data loss occurs when schema validation is bypassed, causing malformed records to be ingested and corrupting downstream ML models.
Duplicate records are ingested when deduplication logic fails, leading to skewed model training data and inaccurate predictions.
Imbalanced partitions occur when partitioning logic does not account for data distribution, causing performance bottlenecks during data retrieval.
metrics: incoming data against a predefined schema using JSON Schema draft-07
